tools.name: search
tools.description: Search MCP servers by server title/name, description, or by the tools they provide. Accepts natural language capability queries like 'send emails', 'search the web', 'create pull requests', or direct server names like 'GitHub' or 'Stripe'. Results are ranked by relevance: title match first, then too

tools.name: inspect
tools.description: Get full metadata for any MCP server. Accepts reverse-DNS name, endpoint URL, or plain keyword. Includes payment protocol detection.

tools.name: catalog
tools.description: List all registries being searched with live status and total server counts.

tools.name: checkout
tools.description: Detect payment protocol for any MCP server and route to the correct payment execution. Supports x402 (USDC on Base), MPP (Stripe), and prepaid API keys.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "search" is 725 chars, far longer than this server's median (142). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Search MCP servers by server title/name, description, or by the tools they provide. Accepts natural language capability queries like 'send emails', 'search the …

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search, inspect · Private data: checkout · Public sink: search · Destructive: search.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search, inspect · Private data: checkout · Public sink: search · Destructive: search

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
